name: Indian Oil Corporation (IOC)
description: India's largest commercial enterprise by revenue and the flagship national oil company. Operates the country's biggest refining network (80.7 MTPA), a vast fuel retail network (35,000+ outlets), and manages a significant share of India's strategic petroleum reserves. Central to India's energy security.
objectives: Ensure uninterrupted fuel supply across India despite supply disruption
Manage crude procurement costs and protect refining margins
Maintain strategic petroleum reserve levels for national energy security
Protect financial stability and shareholder value under extreme cost pressure
constraints: Government-regulated retail fuel pricing — cannot fully pass through costs
88% import dependency on crude oil — limited domestic alternatives
Pipeline and logistics infrastructure designed around Gulf crude grades
Refinery configuration optimized for specific crude baskets — switching grades has yield penalties
Strategic petroleum reserves cover only ~9.5 days of consumption

key_risks: Crude oil price volatility and supply disruption
Refinery utilisation drop if crude supply constrained
